topic: Union Executive — Prime Minister & Council of Ministers

questions.text: The Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to which of the following under the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 75(3) explicitly states that the Council of Ministers shall be collectively responsible to the 'House of the People' — i.e., the Lok Sabha. The government must maintain the confidence of the Lok Sabha at all times. A no-confidence motion can only be moved in Lok Sabha, not Rajya Sabha.
questions.source: SSC CGL — collective responsibility to Lok Sabha is one of the most frequently asked parliamentary government questions

questions.text: The 91st Constitutional Amendment Act (2003) restricted the size of the Council of Ministers to what percentage of the total strength of the lower House?
questions.explanation: The 91st Amendment (2003) inserted a provision that the total number of ministers, including the Prime Minister, in the Council of Ministers shall not exceed 15% of the total strength of the House of the People (Lok Sabha). The amendment also provides that the minimum number of ministers shall not be less than 12.
questions.source: SSC CGL — 91st Amendment and the 15% cap is a standard amendment-specific PYQ

questions.text: Which Article of the Constitution lays down that the Council of Ministers with the Prime Minister as head shall aid and advise the President?
questions.explanation: Article 74(1) states: 'There shall be a Council of Ministers with the Prime Minister at the head to aid and advise the President who shall, in the exercise of his functions, act in accordance with such advice.' This is the constitutional foundation of cabinet government in India.
questions.source: SSC CGL — Article 74 identification is a recurring Polity question

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct hierarchy within the Council of Ministers, from highest to lowest rank?
options: Deputy Ministers → Ministers of State → Cabinet Ministers
Cabinet Ministers → Deputy Ministers → Ministers of State
Cabinet Ministers → Ministers of State → Deputy Ministers
Ministers of State → Cabinet Ministers → Deputy Ministers
questions.explanation: The three tiers in descending order of rank: Cabinet Ministers (senior ministers who head major ministries and attend Cabinet meetings) → Ministers of State (can be with independent charge or attached to Cabinet Ministers) → Deputy Ministers (assist Cabinet Ministers and do not attend Cabinet meetings).
questions.source: SSC CGL — ministerial hierarchy and CoM structure is a standard GK question pattern

questions.text: Which Prime Minister of India served for a decade without ever winning a Lok Sabha election, remaining a Rajya Sabha member throughout their tenure?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh served as Prime Minister from 2004 to 2014 as a Rajya Sabha member throughout. He never contested or won a Lok Sabha seat during his entire political career. This was constitutionally valid — the Constitution does not require the PM to be a Lok Sabha member. H.D. Deve Gowda was appointed PM when not an MP, but became a Rajya Sabha member shortly after.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — anticipated based on 'PM from Rajya Sabha' being a common tricky GK question

questions.text: When the Constitution (Article 352) requires that a National Emergency be recommended by the 'Cabinet,' it refers to:
options: The full Council of Ministers including Ministers of State
Only the Cabinet Ministers (senior ministers), not the full Council
The PM alone, who represents the Cabinet
The Cabinet and all members of both Houses of Parliament
questions.explanation: The term 'Cabinet' in Article 352 refers specifically to the senior Cabinet Ministers — not the full Council of Ministers. This is one of the rare instances where the Constitution itself distinguishes 'Cabinet' (inner circle) from 'Council of Ministers' (full body including MoS and Deputy Ministers). A proclamation of Emergency requires a written recommendation signed by the Cabinet, not every minister.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — anticipated based on the Cabinet vs Council of Ministers distinction in Article 352 being a subtle but high-value trap

questions.text: A person who is not a member of Parliament is appointed as Prime Minister. According to the Constitution, within how many months must they become a member of either House?
questions.explanation: Article 75(5) states that a minister who is not a member of either House of Parliament for a period of six consecutive months shall cease to be a minister. This applies to the PM as well — they must become a member of Parliament (either House) within 6 months of assuming office, or they cease to hold office.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — anticipated based on the 6-month rule for non-MP ministers being a recurring trap question

questions.text: Who was the Prime Minister of India who served the shortest tenure — twice — as acting Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Gulzarilal Nanda served as acting Prime Minister twice — for 13 days after Jawaharlal Nehru's death (May–June 1964) and again for 13 days after Lal Bahadur Shastri's death in Tashkent (January 1966). He is the shortest-serving PM in Indian history (on each occasion), though never elected as a full PM in his own right.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — underrated because Gulzarilal Nanda is rarely taught despite his unique constitutional role

questions.text: Article 78 of the Constitution deals with the duties of the Prime Minister with respect to the President. Which of the following is NOT among those duties?
options: To communicate to the President all decisions of the CoM relating to administration
To furnish such information as the President may call for
To submit for CoM consideration any matter the President requires after a single-minister decision
To appoint governors of states on behalf of the President
questions.explanation: Article 78 lists three PM duties: (a) communicate all CoM decisions to the President, (b) furnish information the President calls for, and (c) submit for CoM consideration any matter on which a minister has decided alone, if the President requires it. Appointment of governors is not part of Article 78 — it happens under Article 155, on the PM's recommendation.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — underrated because students know Article 74 but often cannot recall the specific content of Article 78

questions.text: The concept of 'Kitchen Cabinet' in Indian politics refers to:
options: The sub-committee of the Cabinet dealing with food and agriculture
The informal inner circle of the PM's closest political advisers, with no constitutional status
The group of ministers who form the CoM's economic sub-committee
questions.explanation: The 'Kitchen Cabinet' is an informal concept — a small group of the PM's most trusted personal advisers and political confidants who meet informally and wield real influence. It has no constitutional basis, no defined membership, and no formal procedures. Every PM has had one. It is distinct from the Cabinet Committee on Security or any constitutional body.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — underrated because Kitchen Cabinet appears in mock tests but students confuse it with formal bodies

questions.text: Which of the following statements about 'collective responsibility' of the Council of Ministers is CORRECT?
options: Each minister is individually responsible; cabinet decisions do not bind all ministers
All ministers must resign if the Lok Sabha passes a no-confidence motion against the government
A no-confidence motion can be introduced in either House of Parliament
The Cabinet is collectively responsible to both Houses of Parliament
questions.explanation: Under collective responsibility (Article 75(3)), the entire Council of Ministers — not just the PM — must resign if the Lok Sabha passes a vote of no confidence. Additionally: a no-confidence motion can only be initiated in Lok Sabha (not Rajya Sabha); all Cabinet decisions bind all ministers collectively; and a minister who disagrees must resign before opposing the decision publicly.
questions.source: Collective responsibility is one of the most consistently tested parliamentary government concepts across SSC papers

questions.text: Which of the following is the key constitutional difference between the 'Cabinet' and the 'Council of Ministers'?
options: Cabinet includes all ministers; Council of Ministers includes only senior ministers
Council of Ministers includes all ministers; Cabinet is only the senior Cabinet Ministers
Cabinet and Council of Ministers are different names for the same body
The Cabinet is chaired by the President; the Council of Ministers is chaired by the PM
questions.explanation: The Council of Ministers is the full body — Cabinet Ministers + Ministers of State + Deputy Ministers. The Cabinet is the senior inner circle — only Cabinet Ministers. All Cabinet Ministers are part of the CoM, but not all CoM members are Cabinet Ministers. Article 74 mentions 'Council of Ministers'; Article 352 specifically uses 'Cabinet' when referring to Emergency recommendations.
questions.source: Cabinet vs Council of Ministers distinction recurs regularly in SSC Polity as a conceptual distinction question

questions.text: Which was the first woman to serve as Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the first (and so far only) woman Prime Minister of India. She served two terms: 1966–1977 and 1980–1984. She was assassinated in October 1984. Sucheta Kripalani was the first woman Chief Minister (Uttar Pradesh, 1963). Pratibha Patil was the first woman President (2007–2012).
questions.source: First woman PM is a standard static GK question; the PM vs President distinction (Indira Gandhi vs Pratibha Patil) is the standard distractor
